title: Dropout Programs - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Youth Services > Dropout Programs 5 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF IMPACT NW IMPACT NW BRENTWOOD-DARLINGTON COMMUNITY CENTER SUN YOUTH ADVOCACY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides school-linked, age-appropriate, and culturally specific academic support that includes activities such as tutoring, mentoring, reading club, gender groups, and conflict resolution classes; case management, family engagement, and skill-building groups. 7211 SE 62nd Avenue Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Youth ages 12-17 who are at risk of academic failure Hours: Varies by service Email (503) 867-2188 Get Directions Visit Website More Details METROPOLITAN FAMILY SERVICE SUN YOUTH ADVOCACY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides school-linked, age-appropriate, and culturally specific academic support activities such as tutoring, mentoring, reading clubs, gender groups, conflict resolution classes, case management, family engagement, and skill-building groups. 1700 NW Civic Drive Suite 200 Gresham, OR 97080 Eligibility: Grades 5-12 who are at risk of academic failure and their families Hours: Varies Email (971) 506-2609 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON YOUTH CHALLENGE HIGH SCHOOL OREGON YOUTH CHALLENGE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accredited, tuition free, alternative high school serving youth. Offers a 22 week residential phase and a 12 month post-residential phase, where academically at-risk students can earn 8-10 high school credits and/or their GED or high school diploma upon completion. 23861 Dodds Road Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: -Ages 15-18 (must be 16 by graduation) -Legal resident of the US and State of Oregon -Academically deficient (behind in high school credits), at-risk of not graduating or a high school drop-out -Free from felony convictions and no open violations for adult status offenses -Physically and mentally capable of participating Hours: Office staff available Monday-Friday 7:30am-4pm, OYCP operates 24/7 Email (541) 317-9623 223 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUTSIDE IN OUTSIDE IN EMPLOYMENT RESOURCE CENTER EDUCATION AND EMPLOYMENT RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers GED preparation and testing, GED/high school credit recovery and tutoring that is Portland Public School accredited, work readiness training, job search help, resume help, college preparation, and help with post-secondary education and training. "Urban Ed" is a drop-in, student-centered alternative school for youth who have dropped out or are at risk of dropping out of school. Work experiences are available at the Virginia Woof (VW) Dog Daycare and Youth Job Training Program. Child care is also available for established clients. 1132 SW 13th Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 18-24 who are low-income, homeless, or at risk of homelessness Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday 10am-1pm and 2pm-5pm, Wednesday/Friday 10am-1pm Email (503) 445-0976 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND COMMUNITY COLLEGE PORTLAND COMMUNITY COLLEGE SOUTHEAST CAMPUS YES TO COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a GED preparation program for students who do not have a high school diploma. Also, offers a high school completion program where students earn a high school diploma and college credits that can be applied towards an associate degree, certificate program, or transferred to a four-year college or university. Provides English for Speakers of Other Languages (ESOL) test preparation. 2305 SE 82nd Avenue Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Ages 16-20 in need of an alternative education placement. Requires school district approval. Hours: Hours vary Email (971) 722-6293 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMPACT NW SUN YOUTH ADVOCACY PROGRAM METROPOLITAN FAMILY SERVICE SUN YOUTH ADVOCACY PROGRAM OREGON YOUTH CHALLENGE HIGH SCHOOL OREGON YOUTH CHALLENGE PROGRAM OUTSIDE IN EDUCATION AND EMPLOYMENT RESOURCE CENTER PORTLAND COMMUNITY COLLEGE YES TO COLLEGE
